chin_falam.barry_harris_1929_2021_codified_bebop_: Barry Harris (1929-2021) codified bebop harmony. His system uses 6th chords instead of 7ths and adds diminished passing chords between scale degrees for smooth voice leading.
chin_falam.attack_time_to_reach_full_volume_decay: Attack: time to reach full volume. Decay: time to fall to sustain. Sustain: held volume level. Release: fade-out time after key release.
chin_falam.a_scale_is_an_ordered_set_of_pitches_m: A scale is an ordered set of pitches. Modes are rotations of the scale starting on different degrees. Each creates a different emotional mood.
chin_falam.chords_are_built_by_stacking_intervals: Chords are built by stacking intervals (3rds). Major uses a major 3rd + minor 3rd. Minor reverses them. Extended chords add 7ths, 9ths, and beyond.
chin_falam.kicks_use_a_sine_oscillator_with_a_pit: Kicks use a sine oscillator with a pitch sweep. Snares combine noise (filtered) + tone body. Hi-hats use high-pass filtered noise bursts.
chin_falam.4_4_4_quarter_note_beats_per_bar_most_: 4/4 = 4 quarter-note beats per bar (most common). 3/4 = waltz feel. 6/8 = compound duple. 5/4 and 7/8 create asymmetric, exotic grooves.
chin_falam.keys_arranged_by_ascending_5ths_clockw: Keys arranged by ascending 5ths clockwise. Adjacent keys share 6 of 7 notes. Moving clockwise adds sharps; counter-clockwise adds flats.
chin_falam.every_musical_sound_contains_overtones: Every musical sound contains overtones above the fundamental. Their relative strengths determine timbre — why a trumpet sounds different from a flute at the same pitch.
chin_falam.an_interval_is_the_distance_between_tw: An interval is the distance between two pitches, measured in semitones. The frequency ratio determines consonance (simple ratios like 3:2 sound smooth).
chin_falam.plays_chord_notes_one_at_a_time_in_seq: Plays chord notes one at a time in sequence. Up = low to high, Down = high to low. Creates flowing patterns from static chords.
chin_falam.a_filter_removes_frequencies_above_low: A filter removes frequencies above (lowpass), below (highpass), or around (bandpass) a cutoff frequency. Resonance (Q) amplifies frequencies near cutoff, creating a peak.
chin_falam.tremolo_periodic_volume_changes_amplit: Tremolo: periodic volume changes (amplitude modulation). Vibrato: periodic pitch changes (frequency modulation). Both controlled by an LFO (Low Frequency Oscillator).
chin_falam.discovered_in_1983_a_noise_burst_feeds: Discovered in 1983. A noise burst feeds into a very short delay line with feedback. The delay time determines pitch. A filter in the feedback loop controls brightness and decay. Naturally simulates plucked strings.
chin_falam.the_sequencer_is_a_composition_tool_ea: The sequencer is a composition tool. Each step represents a beat subdivision. The melody row sets pitched notes, drums add rhythm. Musical notation shows what you create.
chin_falam.notes_on_lines_and_spaces_represent_pi: Notes on lines and spaces represent pitches. Duration is shown by note shape: whole (○), half (\u{1D15E}), quarter (♩), eighth (♪). The staff uses 5 lines.
chin_falam.equal_temperament_12_notes_per_octave_: Equal temperament: 12 notes per octave, ratio 2^(1/12). A4 = 440 Hz; A5 = 880 Hz. Bach’s Well-Tempered Clavier (1722) sold the world on this compromise. Every note you play here lives on that grid.
chin_falam.scales_the_major_minor_modal_grammar: Scales — the major + minor + modal grammar
chin_falam.major_w_w_h_w_w_w_h_minor_w_h_w_w_h_w_: Major: W-W-H-W-W-W-H. Minor: W-H-W-W-H-W-W. The 7 modes (Ionian, Dorian, Phrygian, Lydian, Mixolydian, Aeolian, Locrian) come from rotating the major-scale interval pattern.
chin_falam.triads_root_third_fifth_major_4_3_semi: Triads: root + third + fifth. Major (4+3 semitones), minor (3+4), diminished (3+3), augmented (4+4). 7th chords add another third. Pop music = ~6 chords on repeat; jazz uses extensions and substitutions.
chin_falam.sustained_chord_textures_the_bed_under: Sustained chord textures — the bed under almost every cinematic score. Try iv-V-i in minor, or i-VI-VII for that Hans Zimmer feel. Drones in fifths underpin Indian classical and bagpipe traditions.
chin_falam.most_popular_music_sits_at_4_4_90_130_: Most popular music sits at 4/4, 90-130 BPM. Hip-hop ~85-95, house 120-130, drum-and-bass ~170 (half-time feel). The kick on 1+3, snare on 2+4 = backbeat — the foundation of rock + pop.
chin_falam.theory_the_math_behind_the_sound: Theory — the math behind the sound
chin_falam.pythagoras_500_bce_discovered_consonan: Pythagoras (~500 BCE) discovered consonance from integer frequency ratios: octave 2:1, fifth 3:2, fourth 4:3. Helmholtz extended this in 1862. Sound = compression waves; music = patterns we agree to call beautiful.
chin_falam.full_chord_grid_strum_plate_and_voice_: Full chord grid, strum plate, and voice presets
chin_falam.frequency_modulation_fm_synthesis_uses: Frequency Modulation (FM) synthesis uses one oscillator (modulator) to rapidly change the frequency of another (carrier). The modulator:carrier ratio determines the harmonic content. Simple ratios (1:1, 2:1, 3:1) produce harmonic tones. Complex ratios produce metallic, bell-like, or inharmonic sounds. FM was popularized by the Yamaha DX7 in 1983.
chin_falam.supersaw_stacks_multiple_sawtooth_osci: SuperSaw stacks multiple sawtooth oscillators slightly detuned from each other. This creates a huge, wide, shimmering sound — the signature of EDM, trance, and modern pop. More voices = thicker sound but uses more CPU. Detuning controls how far apart the voices are tuned — more detune = wider/more chorus-like, less = tighter/more focused.
chin_falam.an_arpeggiator_plays_the_notes_of_a_ch: 🔬 An arpeggiator plays the notes of a chord one at a time in sequence. "Up" goes low to high, creating ascending energy. "Down" descends for a cascading feel. Rate controls speed (lower = faster). Gate controls how long each note rings (100% = legato, 10% = staccato). Try rate=100ms + 4 octaves + up pattern for a classic trance arp!
chin_falam.sing_speak_or_play_an_instrument_hear_: Sing, speak, or play an instrument — hear effects in real-time!
chin_falam.removes_frequencies_above_lowpass_or_b: Removes frequencies above (lowpass) or below (highpass) a threshold. Like cupping your hand over your mouth — it removes high frequencies, making sound "darker."
chin_falam.creates_echoes_by_copying_the_signal_a: Creates echoes by copying the signal and playing it back after a short time. Feedback controls how many echoes repeat.
chin_falam.simulates_room_acoustics_by_creating_t: Simulates room acoustics by creating thousands of tiny echoes that blend together. Small room = short reverb, cathedral = long reverb.
chin_falam.clips_the_waveform_peaks_turning_smoot: Clips the waveform peaks, turning smooth sine waves into harsh, angular shapes. This adds new harmonics that weren't in the original sound.
chin_falam.duplicates_the_signal_with_a_slight_co: Duplicates the signal with a slight, continuously varying delay. This simulates multiple voices/instruments playing together slightly out of time.
chin_falam.rapidly_varies_the_volume_up_and_down_: Rapidly varies the volume up and down, creating a pulsating effect. Common in surf guitar and organ music.
chin_falam.use_headphones_when_monitoring_to_avoi: Use headphones when monitoring to avoid feedback loops! The mic routes through all the effects above (filter, delay, reverb, etc.) — adjust them to hear how each one transforms your voice or instrument.
chin_falam.connect_your_microphone_to_run_your_vo: Connect your microphone to run your voice or instrument through the effects chain in real-time.
chin_falam.see_your_voice_as_a_waveform_frequency: See your voice as a waveform + frequency spectrum. Hear how filter, delay, reverb, distortion, and chorus physically change sound waves.
chin_falam.touch_drag_x_pitch_y_filter_effect: Touch & drag — X = pitch, Y = filter/effect
chin_falam.the_xy_pad_maps_your_finger_mouse_posi: The XY pad maps your finger/mouse position to musical parameters in real-time. The X axis controls pitch (left = low, right = high). The Y axis controls the filter cutoff frequency (top = bright, bottom = dark). This is similar to instruments like the Korg Kaoss Pad, which revolutionized live electronic music performance by turning 2D gestures into sound.
chin_falam.xy_performance_pad_left_right_is_pitch: XY performance pad. Left–right is pitch, up–down is filter brightness. Use the arrow keys to move and play a note; press Escape or Space to stop.
chin_falam.sequencer_drum_pads_notation_samples_m: Sequencer, drum pads, notation, samples & more →
chin_falam.click_cells_to_place_notes_visual_melo: Click cells to place notes — visual melody editor
chin_falam.chord_progressions_create_emotional_jo: Chord progressions create emotional journeys. The I chord (tonic) feels like 'home.' The V chord (dominant) creates tension that wants to resolve back to I. The vi chord (relative minor) adds sadness or depth. The IV chord (subdominant) moves away from home gently. The I-V-vi-IV progression is estimated to appear in over 70% of pop songs because it balances these emotions perfectly.
chin_falam.roman_numerals_i_ii_iii_iv_v_vi_vii_re: Roman numerals (I, ii, iii, IV, V, vi, vii°) represent scale degrees. Uppercase = major chord. Lowercase = minor chord.
chin_falam.add_melody_notes_in_the_grid_above: Add melody notes in the grid above ↑
chin_falam.click_melody_cells_in_the_grid_above_t: Click melody cells in the grid above to see notation here
chin_falam.click_challenge_me_to_practice_rhythm_: Click "Challenge me!" to practice rhythm patterns
chin_falam.upload_wav_mp3_files_for_custom_pads_s: Upload WAV/MP3 files for custom pads. Samples clear on page refresh.
chin_falam.chain_patterns_into_a_full_song: Chain patterns into a full song
chin_falam.add_current_pattern_as_a_section: Add current pattern as a section
chin_falam.create_a_drum_pattern_in_the_grid_abov: Create a drum pattern in the grid above, then click "+ Add Current as Section" to start building a song.
chin_falam.sections_intro_verse_chorus_bridge_cho: Sections: Intro → Verse → Chorus → Bridge → Chorus → Outro
chin_falam.no_saved_beats_yet_create_a_pattern_an: No saved beats yet. Create a pattern and click Save!
chin_falam.harmonypad_creates_warm_pure_tones_by_: HarmonyPad creates warm, pure tones by blending sine and triangle waves. Choose a voice, pick a chord from the grid below, then strum the plate!
chin_falam.chord_grid_tap_to_select_space_to_stru: 🎶 Chord Grid — tap to select, Space to strum
chin_falam.strum_plate_tap_or_drag_across: 🎸 Strum Plate — tap or drag across
chin_falam.hear_see_the_waveform_and_learn_the_st: Hear, see the waveform, and learn the story of each instrument
chin_falam.this_is_how_different_instruments_get_: This is how different instruments get their unique sound! A flute is mostly harmonic 1 (sine). A clarinet has odd harmonics (1,3,5,7). A violin has all harmonics. Click to build your own timbre!
chin_falam.karplus_strong_synthesis_creates_reali: Karplus-Strong synthesis creates realistic plucked string sounds using a short noise burst fed into a delay line with filtered feedback. Adjust brightness (initial noise color) and damping (sustain length) to shape the string character.
chin_falam.what_type_of_chord_do_you_hear: What type of chord do you hear?
chin_falam.identify_each_note_in_the_4_note_melod: Identify each note in the 4-note melody:
chin_falam.roughly_equal_harmonics_full_natural_t: Roughly equal harmonics — full / natural timbre.
chin_falam.adjust_fundamental_mid_and_high_harmon: Adjust fundamental, mid, and high harmonic levels. Widget classifies one of five discrete timbres. No score, no reveal — sweep and notice.
chin_falam.hypothesis_free_text_which_harmonic_le: Hypothesis (free text): Which harmonic level dictates "bright" vs "warm"?
chin_falam.hold_two_sliders_steady_move_one_watch: Hold two sliders steady. Move one. Watch.
chin_falam.find_two_settings_producing_the_same_t: Find two settings producing the same timbre. What proportions do they share?
chin_falam.real_instruments_have_characteristic_s: Real instruments have characteristic spectra. Investigate why flute sounds different than violin.
chin_falam.i_understand_explain_in_own_words: I understand — explain in own words
chin_falam.explain_how_harmonic_content_determine: Explain how harmonic content determines timbre perception.
chin_falam.design_note_discrete_5_class_timbre_ma: Design note: discrete 5-class timbre marker; no acoustic fidelity score; no reveal — by design.
chin_falam.using_simple_everyday_words_and_short_: using simple everyday words and short sentences, no jargon
chin_falam.for_a_5th_grade_student_brief_and_frie: for a 5th grade student, brief and friendly
chin_falam.for_a_student_who_already_knows_some_m: for a student who already knows some music theory
chin_falam.click_explain_for_the_ai_tutor_to_desc: Click “Explain” for the AI tutor to describe what this view teaches at your chosen reading level.
chin_falam.harmonic_series_why_instruments_sound_: 🎵 Harmonic Series — Why instruments sound different
